# Partijgedrag.be

> Partijgedrag.be ("party behavior") is an independent, non-commercial website that tracks and visualizes how the Belgian Chamber of Representatives (Belgische Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers / Chambre des représentants de Belgique) and its 150 members vote. It aggregates official parliamentary data from dekamer.be into a clear, browsable, and machine-readable overview of votes, legislative dossiers, members of parliament, party groups, meetings, topics, the federal government, and the lobby register.

The site is available in Dutch (default), French, and English. It has no affiliation with, or funding from, the Chamber, and is not commercial. Some pages offer an AI-summarized "beknopte versie" (concise version) of official texts; such AI-generated content is always visually marked (underlined in purple) and is not a verbatim statement by the people involved.

## Core sections

- [Members of parliament](https://partijgedrag.be/members/): Overview of all 150 sitting members, searchable by name, party, or fraction. Individual profiles at `/members/{first-name}-{last-name}/` show voting behavior, oral questions, and submitted dossiers per member.
- [Fractions (party groups)](https://partijgedrag.be/fractions/): Overview of all party groups represented in the Chamber. Individual profiles at `/fractions/{fraction-slug}/` show a fraction's voting behavior and dossiers.
- [Dossiers](https://partijgedrag.be/dossiers/): Legislative dossiers (bills, proposals) from submission to final vote. Individual pages at `/sessions/{session_id}/dossiers/{dossier_id}/` list related votes, involved members, and plenary treatment.
- [Meetings](https://partijgedrag.be/meetings/): Overview of plenary and committee meetings. Plenary reports at `/sessions/{session_id}/meetings/p{meeting_id}/`, committee reports at `/sessions/{session_id}/meetings/c{meeting_id}/`. Individual roll-call votes live at `/sessions/{session_id}/meetings/p{meeting_id}/votes/{vote_id}/`.
- [Topics](https://partijgedrag.be/topics/): Dossiers and votes grouped by theme (e.g. climate, budget, justice), showing how parties position themselves per subject. Individual topic pages at `/topics/{topic-slug}/` (subtopics nested under their parent topic).
- [Commissions](https://partijgedrag.be/commissions/): Overview of parliamentary committees, their chairs, and members.
- [Federal government](https://partijgedrag.be/government/): Current ministers of the Belgian federal government and their portfolios.
- [Lobby register](https://partijgedrag.be/lobby/): Registered lobbyists and organizations active in parliament, grouped by sector.
- [Blog](https://partijgedrag.be/blog/): Articles explaining how the Chamber and this website work, plus site updates.

## About & policies

- [About](https://partijgedrag.be/about/): Explains the site's purpose, data sources, FAQ, and data/usage terms (most parliamentary data has no copyright under Belgian law and may be freely reproduced).
- [Privacy](https://partijgedrag.be/privacy/): Privacy policy; the site collects minimal personal data.
- [Feedback](https://partijgedrag.be/feedback/): Form to report data errors or suggest improvements.
- [Newsletter](https://partijgedrag.be/newsletter/): Subscribe for updates on parliamentary voting behavior.

## Optional

- [Sitemap](https://partijgedrag.be/sitemap.xml): Full, up-to-date list of every page on the site (all members, fractions, dossiers, meetings, votes, and topics).
- [Original source](https://dekamer.be): Official website of the Belgian Chamber of Representatives, the primary source for most data on this site.
